title: senecio hispidulus var. dissectus
intro: Senecio hispidulus var. dissectus, also known as the Jacob's Ladder or dissected woolly groundsel, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Senecio hispidulus var. dissectus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
